page: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This instance features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_NI. The hardware configuration is bundled with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, alongside 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 10% of its class. However, multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores align with the middle 50% average, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is robust, with PassMark memory scores and large-block write bandwidth ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also perform strongly. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput fall into the bottom 25%. Large-model LLM inference is limited, with 7B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10%, though small-model inference remains average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of memory and NVMe storage relative to its shared vCPU allocation, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, sustained multi-core compute. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-effective footprint for caching layers, memory-bound applications, and moderate database operations, despite a lower benchmark score in raw database transaction tests. It is well-suited for memory-intensive background processing, Redis deployments, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial local NVMe storage without heavy multi-threaded compute demands.
description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC processor with 4 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth, though multi-core performance and database operations remain average to weak due to the shared CPU allocation. This server provides a cost-effective profile for memory-heavy workloads that benefit from fast local storage but do not require dedicated compute resources. It is best suited for caching layers, Redis deployments, memory-bound background tasks, and general-purpose applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 4 shared vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe storage, offering top-tier single-core performance and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
960 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth
Optimized for memory-bound applications, Redis, and caching layers
